texts.orig: Abstract
texts.text: Abstract

texts.label: text
texts.orig: One deals with r -regular bipartite graphs with 2 n vertices. In a previous paper Butera, Pernici, and the author have introduced a quantity d ( i ), a function of the number of i -matchings, and conjectured that as n goes to infinity the fraction of graphs that satisfy ∆ k d ( i ) ≥ 0, for all k and i , approaches 1. Here ∆ is the finite difference operator. This conjecture we called the 'graph positivity conjecture'. 'Weak graph positivity' is the conjecture that for each i and k the probability that ∆ k d ( i ) ≥ 0 goes to 1 as n goes to infinity. Here we prove this for the range of parameters where r ≤ 10 , i + k ≤ 100 , k ≤ 27. or i + k ≤ 29 all r . A formalism of Wanless as systematized by Pernici is central to this effort.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: It is sad to know that Robin Chapman is now dead. He did not complete preparation of A Stirling number identity of which he sent me a rough draft. Nor do we know if he would have sought to publish it. The proof we present follows the lines of his rough draft. This presentation is centered about proving eq.(C.1) below. It was I think particularly clever of Robin Chapman to realize this was the 'right' equivalent formulation of our Theorem A.2 ( presented as a conjecture on the web ) to address.
